title: Barclays Lowers Unity Software Stock Price Target to $28 from $35
text: Barclays lowered Unity Software's stock price target to $28 from $35. Ross Sandler from Barclays has maintained a Hold rating since February 27, 2024, with a $33 price target. The downgrade appears to be recent, as Unity Software reported mixed fourth-quarter 2025 results with revenue and EBITDA surpassing guidance, but the company's first-quarter 2026 guidance did not meet expectations, with midpoint revenue and EBITDA projections falling 1.5% and 8% below consensus estimates. Additionally, Unity's decision to shut down its ironSource ad operation led other analysts to lower their price targets as well, with BTIG reducing its target from $60.00 to $41.00 and Citizens reducing its from $50.00 to $37.00. This reflects investor concern about Unity's advertising business strategy and the impact of the IronSource divestment on near-term financial performance.
